TSLA: Matthews International Obtains Important Clarity On Matthews' Right to Sell DBE Equipment. Matthews International (NASDAQ GSM: MATW) has received further clarity regarding its right to sell proprietary dry battery electrode (DBE) solutions, with an arbitrator denying most of Tesla's claims to broadly prohibit Matthews from selling its DBE technology. The decision includes only a narrow injunction on certain parts, which Matthews states will not materially impede its operations due to readily available replacements. This ruling reinforces Matthews' ability to continue advancing its DBE technology and supplies to customers, supported by foundational patents.

Tesla’s Former Supplier Says It Won Lawsuit Against EV Maker But VP Disputes Claim. Matthews International Corporation announced that an arbitrator sided with them in a lawsuit against Tesla, denying Tesla's request to restrict Matthews from selling its dry battery electrode technology. However, Tesla VP Bonne Eggleston disputed this, claiming Tesla obtained a permanent injunction against Matthews for intellectual property theft and that Matthews is "publishing complete nonsense." Tesla had sued Matthews for allegedly stealing trade secrets related to battery manufacturing and sharing them with competitors, seeking over $1 billion in damages.

Elon Musk's Tesla Loses Another Senior Executive As Finance VP Sendil Palani Steps Down After 17 Years. Tesla's Vice President of Finance, Sendil Palani, has announced his departure from the company after 17 years. This marks another high-profile executive exit for the Elon Musk-led automaker, following other departures like the Robotaxi backend director and VP of IT and AI Infrastructure. Despite these changes and rising competition from BYD, Tesla recently saw a 10% surge in February registrations in Europe.

Tesla Finance VP Exits in Latest Departure for Musk’s EV Maker. Sendil Palani, Tesla's Vice President of Finance, is leaving the company after 17 years, marking another high-profile departure from the electric vehicle maker. His exit comes as Tesla pivots its strategy towards artificial intelligence and robotics. Palani held various roles in finance, engineering, and manufacturing during his two stints at Tesla, serving as VP of finance since 2021.

Tesla Loses Director Behind Robotaxi Backend: What Do Prediction Markets Say?. Tesla has seen the departure of Thomas Dmytryk, an 11-year veteran who developed the software infrastructure for its robotaxi service, just weeks before the anticipated start of Cybercab production. This exit follows other key departures in 2026, raising concerns about the company's ability to meet ambitious robotaxi and vehicle delivery targets. Prediction markets reflect skepticism, with low probabilities for Tesla achieving its stated goals for the robotaxi launch in California and vehicle deliveries.

Lucid (LCID) and Rivian (RIVN) Stocks Jump as Oil Rises but Tesla (TSLA) Stock Lags. Lucid (LCID) and Rivian (RIVN) stocks saw a sharp increase due to a surge in oil prices caused by Middle East tensions, pushing WTI crude to around $86 per barrel. In contrast, Tesla (TSLA) stock lagged, weighed down by a new federal probe into its Full Self-Driving (FSD) system and intensifying competition from Chinese EV manufacturer BYD. Despite the short-term lift for Lucid and Rivian, long-term challenges remain for Lucid, while Rivian shows some positive momentum with its first quarter of positive gross profit in Q4 2025.

Tesla's FSD Safety Metrics 'Sharply Deteriorating,' Says Analyst. Analyst Gordon Johnson of GLJ Research has highlighted a sharp deterioration in Tesla's Full Self-Driving (FSD) safety metrics, citing third-party FSD Community Tracker data. The critical "city miles to critical disengagement" metric for FSD v14.2 significantly dropped to 809 from a peak of 4,109 with v14.1, which is notably lower than Waymo's 30,000 miles before removing safety drivers. Johnson has also questioned the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration's stance on this issue.
TGT: Korea Investment CORP Acquires 1,470,295 Shares of Kenvue Inc. $KVUE. Korea Investment CORP significantly increased its stake in Kenvue Inc. by 139.5%, acquiring an additional 1,470,295 shares to bring its total to 2,524,461 shares. This positions Kenvue as a notable holding for institutions, with 97.64% of its stock owned by institutional investors and 0.17% by insiders. The article also highlights recent insider buying and analyst ratings, with Kenvue receiving an average "Hold" rating and an average price target of $19.58.

Costco to open 28 new stores this year. Any in Florida?. Costco plans to open 28 new stores in fiscal year 2026, with targets for 30+ openings annually in the coming years. While several new locations are scheduled across the US and Canada in the near future, Florida has new stores planned for St. Lucie and Sanford, with a large distribution center opening soon in Port St. Lucie. The article also touches on Costco's recent sales growth, membership income rise, and changes made in 2025 like price drops and expanded hours, alongside updates on other grocery chains expanding in Florida.

Target Corp. (TGT) jumps 6.7% as firm turns to AI for growth. Target (TGT) saw its stock jump by 6.7% due to investor optimism regarding the company's new AI initiatives aimed at boosting growth. The retail giant plans to leverage artificial intelligence to improve supply chain efficiency, personalize customer experiences, and analyze market trends. This strategic shift towards AI is expected to streamline operations and enhance Target's competitive edge in the retail sector.

e.l.f. Beauty (NYSE:ELF) Shares Down 10.2% - Here's What Happened. e.l.f. Beauty (NYSE:ELF) shares dropped 10.2% on Monday, trading at $74.00, despite the company beating quarterly earnings estimates and providing strong FY2026 guidance. Analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" consensus rating with a target price of $119.13, yet the stock is trading below its 50-day and 200-day moving averages. Institutional investors hold a significant 92.44% of the company's stock.

Form 424B2 BARCLAYS BANK PLC. This SEC filing (Form 424B2) from Barclays Bank PLC details an offering of $1,275,000 Autocallable Contingent Coupon Barrier Notes due March 10, 2027. These notes are linked to the common stock of Lululemon Athletica Inc., Nike, Inc. (Class B), and Target Corporation, and do not guarantee principal repayment or interest. The document outlines the terms, risks, hypothetical scenarios for payments, and tax considerations for investors.
DIS: Exploring Business Segments Across The Walt Disney Company Australia and New Zealand. The Walt Disney Company's diverse operations in Australia and New Zealand encompass various business segments, including corporate support, theatrical distribution, direct-to-consumer streaming with Disney+, ESPN, content sales, and live entertainment. The company also highlights its Disney Studios Australia, consumer products division, Disney Destinations, Marvel Stadium sponsorship, and advertising platforms, demonstrating its broad impact on the region's media and entertainment landscape. These segments aim to fulfill Disney's mission to entertain, inform, and inspire through unparalleled storytelling.

Disney Stock Slides To Start The Week: What's Behind The Weakness? - Walt Disney (NYSE:DIS). Walt Disney Co (NYSE: DIS) shares are trading lower due to investor concerns over fresh escalation in the U.S.–Israel–Iran conflict. The stock is currently below its 20-day, 50-day, and 200-day moving averages, indicating a challenging technical setup. Fears of rising oil prices affecting Disney's cruise line and theme park attendance, alongside broader travel sentiment, are contributing to the weakness.
